news_catalyst.headline: Residual pressure from own Q3 AI revenue guidance miss ($16B vs $17.3B estimate, stock -14% on June 3)
news_catalyst.summary: Broadcom's fiscal Q2 results (June 3) guided Q3 AI revenue at $16B — below the $17.3B consensus — and reiterated its $100B+ FY2027 AI target without raising it. The stock fell 14% on the earnings. Today's -2.41% is residual digestion of that miss, compounded by the weak jobs report adding rate hike pressure. The supply-constraint argument (not enough memory/GPUs to deliver more AI revenue) is the bull narrative, but requires patience as the market waits for Q3 guidance to improve.

technical.summary: AVGO has been consolidating in a $356-380 range following the June 3 SC event (3.40x volume, 80M shares — the 90-day max). Low-volume declines like today (0.83x) within the range maintain a consolidation character. The June 4 SC low around $350-356 is critical support. DCF at $1,406 vs $360 = 74.4% MoS is the largest in the watchlist but must be applied with awareness that it captures the combined semiconductor + VMware segments. Even at a 70% haircut, implied value ($422) is above current price.

wyckoff_narrative: AVGO is in a post-SC consolidation without directional Wyckoff clarity. June 3 Buying Climax (3.40x, 80M shares) established the demand peak. Trading in the $356-380 range with mostly below-average volume shows neither strong accumulation nor distribution. Low-volume declines are constructive within a range. Watch for SOS (high-volume bounce from $350-356 zone) as the re-entry signal.

valuation.summary: DCF $1,406 vs $360 = 74.4% MoS — largest in the watchlist. Captures semiconductor + VMware software combined. Even deeply discounted, intrinsic value is above current price. Patience required until AI guidance improves.

risks: Own guidance miss created the sector reset — further disappointment could drive another leg lower
$356.43 intraday low near the June 3 SC floor; close below $350 is technically negative
Market re-rating requires raised guidance, not just reiteration — next earnings is the catalyst
